column1: You talk to yourself, and are amazed to discover you already know what you're going to say.
column2: 你自言自語，驚訝地發現自己早就知道要說啥了。

column1: Good idea. You carefully remove your lubber and dispose of it in a way that will not be described here.
column2: 好主意。你小心翼翼地脫掉下半身，以本遊戲不便描述的方式處理。

column1: Carefully exploring your leisure suit, you find it stylish but empty.
column2: 仔細檢查你的閒散裝，發現它挺時髦但口袋空空。

column1: Larry! The whole point of this game was to stop doing that!
column2: 賴瑞！這遊戲的重點就是要你停止幹那鬼事！

column1: Sniffing your leisure suit thoroughly, you conclude there's nothing quite like the fragrance of fresh polyester! Ahhh!
column2: 徹底聞了聞你的閒散裝，你得出結論：沒啥比得上新鮮聚酯纖維的香氣！啊～

column1: Licking your leisure suit leads you to the conclusion that polyester doesn't mean good taste!
column2: 舔了舔你的閒散裝，讓你得出結論：聚酯纖維一點代表不了品味！

column1: You pay yourself a few bucks, which you decide to keep in your wallet.
column2: 你給自己幾張鈔票，決定塞進錢包。

column1: You don't need to wind your watch; it's self-winding.
column2: 你不用給手錶上弦；它會自動上弦。

column1: (And it seems lately, you've been winding it plenty!)
column2: （而且看來最近你把它玩得夠兇的！）

column1: The apple tastes delicious. You devour it all.
column2: 蘋果嚐起來挺爽的。你一口氣吞下肚。

column1: Slipping the magic ring onto your finger gives you powers far beyond those of...
column2: 把魔戒套上手指，讓你獲得超越……的力量。

column1: You slam down the shot of cheap booze, then with a flourish, break the glass on the floor!
column2: 你豪氣地灌下一杯廉價烈酒，然後一舞手臂，把杯子砸碎！

column1: (Pretty dramatic, eh?)
column2: （挺戲劇化的，是吧？）

column1: Although it appears to do nothing, aiming the remote control at your groin while rapidly pressing the channel selector does make you feel quite silly.
column2: 雖然看不出啥效果，把遙控器對著褲檔狂按選台鍵確實讓你感覺挺傻的。

column1: You prick yourself with the rose.
column2: 玫瑰花刺到你了。

column1: This is hardly the place for that!
column2: 這地方根本不適合幹那個！

column1: The candy tastes so delicious, you consume it all. Now you're worried about your acne.
column2: 糖果嚐起來香得要死，你一口氣吞光。現在你開始擔心青春痘。

column1: Come on, Larry! Don't lose hope! There's no need to commit suicide... at least, not yet!
column2: 拜託啦，賴瑞！別喪氣！沒必要尋短見……至少現在還不用！

column1: You quickly down all the cheap wine, crumple the box, and toss it away. Your thirst is gone.
column2: 你快速灌完廉價紅酒，揉皺紙盒往外丟。渴意消散。

column1: And your breath is revolting!
column2: 而且你的口氣臭死人！

column1: Pounding yourself with the hammer is ONE idea; it's just not a GOOD idea!
column2: 拿鎚子砸自己算一個想法；只是不是個好想法！

column1: Without thinking, you swallow the entire bottle of pills. "Say, this stuff is ok!" you think.
column2: 不經大腦，你吞下整瓶藥丸。「嘿，這玩意不賴啊！」你這麼想。

column1: But, wait! Soon your breathing becomes heavy. Soon you become horny! Soon you must have relief! You've got to do something, and NOW!
column2: 但是，慢著！很快你開始喘氣。很快你變得春心蕩漾！很快你必須洩慾！你得趕快做點啥，立刻就要！

column1: You wonder about the location of that cute, little dog you met outside.
column2: 你琢磨著那隻你在外面碰到的可愛小狗現在在哪。

column1: You've always loved animals!
column2: 你一直都愛動物！

column1: You wrap the pink ribbon carefully around your head, before deciding the "Shirley Temple look" just isn't your bag!
column2: 你小心地把粉紅色蝴蝶結纏在頭上，然後決定「秀蘭朵露風格」根本不是你的菜！

column1: It's a face only a mother could love!
column2: 這是個只有媽媽才愛得起來的臉！

column1: You stick your finger deep into your ear until that itch goes away. You hope no one is watching!
column2: 你把手指深深插進耳朵裡，直到癢感消散。你希望沒人在看！

column1: It's not much to look at.
column2: 看不出啥特別的。

column1: To whom were you trying to speak?
column2: 你想跟誰說話？

column1: There's nothing to do there.
column2: 那邊沒啥好做的。

column1: You gaily display your bright pink ribbon for all to see.
column2: 你歡樂地展示你鮮豔的粉紅色蝴蝶結。

column1: Kill! Kill! Kill!
column2: 殺！殺！殺！

column1: (Doesn't anybody ---- anymore?)
column2: （現在都沒人幹那個了嗎？）

column1: Try finding some place else to place %s.
column2: 找個別的地方放 %s 吧。

column1: You coyly turn your back and relieve yourself. You quickly feel much better. The hint of a smile crosses your lips.
column2: 你害羞地轉過身去解放一下。你立刻感覺好多了。嘴角浮起一絲微笑。

column1: You really don't want to put your face near that.
column2: 你真的不想把臉湊過去。

column1: No! Don't leave me all alone inside your computer! You can't imagine the things your CPU does to me when you're not watching!
column2: 不行！別丟下我一個人在你的電腦裡！你想不出你不看的時候你的 CPU 對我幹了啥！

column1: Your mouth tastes like drippings from a marathon runner's sock.
column2: 你的嘴巴嚐起來像馬拉松選手的襪子滲出來的東西。

column1: Now you know where old elephants go to die--your mouth!
column2: 現在你知道老象去哪死了──你的嘴巴！

column1: Oh, no! You are now penniless. You hock your leisure suit only to spend the rest of your life wandering from town to town, wondering about what "might have been."
column2: 天啊！你現在身無分文。你典當了閒散裝，只能在城鎮間流浪，不停地想著「要是……就好了」。

column1: POOR Larry!
column2: 可憐的賴瑞！

column1: The rest of your story is simply too sad to tell. Larry, it's time for a little "R & R," -- Restart or Restore!
column2: 你的故事其餘部分實在太悲慘了，不足掛齒。賴瑞，是時候來點「R & R」── 重新開始或讀檔！

column1: Why, look! It's %s.
column2: 咦，看哪！是 %s。

column1: You engage in a short, but personally meaningful conversation with %s.
column2: 你跟 %s 有了一段簡短卻意義重大的對話。

column1: That feels exactly like %s.
column2: 那摸起來活像 %s。

column1: Perhaps %s is not into velveteen S & M.
column2: 也許 %s 不幸好天鵝絨虐戀。

column1: You're a lover, not a fighter!
column2: 你是情種，不是鬥士！

column1: It seems %s just doesn't work with %s.
column2: %s 和 %s 看起來根本不合。

column1: Larry! Don't do that to %s!
column2: 賴瑞！別對 %s 幹那個！

column1: To you, %s has no distinctive smell or taste.
column2: 在你鼻子舌頭裡，%s 沒啥特別的味道。

column1: Score: %d of %d
column2: 得分：%d / %d

column1: I have no use for %s! says %s.
column2: 「我用不著 %s！」%s 說。

column1: You notice a sign on that utility pole.
column2: 你注意到那支電線杆上的標牌。

column1: STOP, PERVERT!!! shouts the cop.  "I wonder who he's yelling at," you think.
column2: 「住手，變態！」警察大喊。「不知道他在罵誰，」你心想。

column1: Glancing down, you realize what he is talking about. Your face flushes.
column2: 低頭一看，你明白他在說啥。你臉紅了。

column1: What is that?!
column2: 那是啥玩意？

column1: Next time, if you insist on wearing your "lubber" in public, you might at least zip your fly!
column2: 下次，如果你執意在公開場合穿你的「衣服」，至少拉上褲檔拉鍊啦！

column1: Suddenly, you have a warm feeling in the front of your pants.
column2: 突然，你褲子前面有種溫暖的感覺。

column1: You're no Trojan, Larry!
column2: 你不是特洛伊英雄啦，賴瑞！

column1: While life may be possible, to you it is no longer worth living.
column2: 雖然活著還是可能，但對你來說已經不值得活了。

column1: You won't be safe in there, Larry!
column2: 你在裡面不安全啦，賴瑞！

column1: You rub your fingerprints into Lefty's window!
column2: 你在李夫帝的窗戶上留下指紋！

column1: (You hope Lefty doesn't see you!)
column2: （你希望李夫帝沒看到你！）

column1: You whistle loudly for a taxi.
column2: 你吹著口哨攔計程車。

column1: You shout, "YO! TAXI!!"
column2: 你大喊：「嘿！計程車！」

column1: You wouldn't touch that pole with a 10-foot pole!
column2: 你連十英尺長的竿子都不想用來碰那根桿子！

column1: You COULD tie yourself to the pole, but what would that accomplish?
column2: 你當然可以把自己綁在桿子上，但那幹嘛？

column1: Take that, you mean old pole, you shout!
column2: 「吃我一拳，死桿子，」你大喊！

column1: There's no way to climb up there from here.
column2: 從這裡根本沒辦法爬上去。

column1: Hark, Juliet! What light from yonder window breaks? Be dat you up 'dere?
column2: 「聽著，茱麗葉！那邊窗戶的亮光是咋回事？是你在上面嗎？」

column1: (You were always weak at memorization!)
column2: （你一直都不擅長背台詞！）

column1: That's far too high for you to hurl anything, Larry!
column2: 那高度根本不是你能丟東西的距離啦，賴瑞！

column1: STOP, PERVERT!!! shouts the cop.  "I wonder who he's yelling at?" you think.
column2: 「住手，變態！」警察大喊。「不知道他在罵誰啦？」你心想。

column1: Now that you're here, you decide you don't really want to look inside.
column2: 現在你來了，卻決定根本不想往裡瞧。

column1: Lefty's wife, Linda, must have made her famous tuna casserole again!
column2: 李夫帝的老婆琳達一定又做了她那套聞名的鮪魚砂鍋！

column1: Don't be so impatient, Larry. Wait until Lefty brings your order.
column2: 別這麼急躁，賴瑞。等李夫帝給你端上菜。

column1: This is the sleaziest bar you've ever been in.
column2: 這是你見過最骯髒下流的酒吧。

column1: (And you've been in quite a few.)
column2: （而且你去過不少。）

column1: Talk to someone in particular.
column2: 跟某個特定的人說話。

column1: This place HAS no taste!
column2: 這地方根本沒品味！

column1: You feel a little light-headed. You are unsure if you'll be able to walk straight.
column2: 你感到有點頭暈目眩。不確定還能不能走直線。

column1: Shaking your head, you finally clear away the cobwebs and are once again able to walk in your standard, studly style.
column2: 搖搖頭，你終於甩掉了腦子裡的蜘蛛絲，恢復了標誌性的濕潤步伐。

column1: (So what was the setup to THAT punch line?)
column2: （那個笑點的前情提要是啥啦？）

column1: You rap loudly on the Naugahyde door.
column2: 你在那扇人造皮革門上狠狠敲門。

column1: That'll be $5.00, please.
column2: 「要五塊錢。」

column1: Yeah, and not just in the financial department, Dead-Beat! he replies, "I'll teach you to try and stiff ol' Lefty! Come with me!"
column2: 「沒錯，而且不止財務上啦，死白癡！」他回嘴，「我要教教你敢賴老李夫帝帳的代價！跟我來！」

column1: You flip five bucks onto the counter.
column2: 你甩了五塊錢在吧檯上。

column1: You delicately sip the wine until it's all gone.
column2: 你優雅地啜飲著紅酒，直到一滴不剩。

column1: You tell Lefty, "I find this impudent and sassy, with the slightest hint of impertinence."
column2: 你對李夫帝說：「這酒挺囂張挺辛辣，隐約帶點不恭。」

column1: This one tastes even better than the last. Don't you think one more seems like a good idea?
column2: 這杯比上一杯更讚。你不覺得再來一杯挺不錯嗎？

column1: Ahhhh.
column2: 啊～

column1: Since your breath spray is now empty, you toss it away.
column2: 既然你的口腔噴霧已經用完，乾脆丟掉。

column1: How can you do that from there?
column2: 你從那裡怎麼幹那個？

column1: True to form, you stick your finger in the coin return slot hoping to find some change. You don't.
column2: 按照慣例，你把手指插進硬幣退出槽希望撈到零錢。沒撈到。

column1: Over the years, a few coin-operated machines have screwed you, but this is scarcely the time nor the place for you to seek revenge!
column2: 這些年來，不少投幣機都坑過你，但現在根本不是復仇的時候和地點！

column1: You slip a buck into the juke box, and search for a suitably sleazy selection.
column2: 你塞一塊錢進自動點唱機，找一首夠下流的歌。

column1: It's not a good idea to pick a fight in a dive like this, Larry!
column2: 在這種低級酒吧裡打架不是個好主意啦，賴瑞！

column1: Hey, buddy, you shout to the man at the end of the bar, "is this the only place in this town to find a little action?"
column2: 「嘿，老兄，」你對吧檯盡頭的哥們大喊，「這鎮上就這一家地方有點活動？」

column1: Hardly, he snorts between belts, "ain'cha never heard o' taxicabs?"
column2: 「才怪，」他邊灌酒邊哼著，「你沒聽過計程車嗎？」

column1: Larry! That's a good way to get yourself killed in a place like this!
column2: 賴瑞！在這種地方那樣幹是找死啊！

column1: Hi there, lovely leg! you say casually. "My name's Larry; Larry Laffer."
column2: 「嗨，美腿，」你隨口說，「我叫賴瑞；賴瑞·拉弗。」

column1: Who cares? she replies sarcastically. "Stop bugging me, or I'll have my boyfriend take care of you when he gets back from the rest room!"
column2: 「誰在乎啊？」她冷嘲熱諷地回嘴，「別煩我，不然等我男友上完廁所回來，他會替我收拾你！」

column1: (Looks like you're operating at your standard level of charm, Larry.)
column2: （看起來你又發揮出標誌性的魅力了，賴瑞。）

column1: Yeah, Larry! Smart thinking. Why waste your precious time on small talk?
column2: 沒錯，賴瑞！聰明想法。何必浪費寶貴時間閒扯？

column1: Larry, this girl's not interested in a manly man like you. Keep hunting. You'll find someone sooner or later....
column2: 賴瑞，這女孩對你這種猛男沒興趣。繼續找。遲早會碰到的……

column1: It's not a good idea to pick a fight in a place like this, Larry!
column2: 在這種地方打架不是個好主意啦，賴瑞！

column1: Larry! Put that thing away!
column2: 賴瑞！把那玩意收起來！

column1: (Besides, he already has a date!)
column2: （況且他已經有約了！）

column1: Excuse me, you say to the skinny man, "is by any chance your last name Sprat?"
column2: 「不好意思，」你對瘦子說，「你姓氏不會叫斯普拉特吧？」

column1: Buzz off, Jerk!
column2: 「滾開，混蛋！」

column1: He continues to concentrate on his overweight buddy, leaving you with nothing to do but find something else to do.
column2: 他繼續專心應酬他那肥胖的哥們，撇下你無所事事。

column1: Excuse me, buddy, you say to the overweight man, "but how many stools are under there?"
column2: 「不好意思，老兄，」你對肥子說，「但你下面踩了幾張凳子啊？」

column1: Jer, er, buzz off, Jerk!
column2: 「傑爾，啊，滾開，混蛋！」

column1: He continues to concentrate on his underweight buddy, leaving you with nothing to do but find something else to do.
column2: 他繼續專心應酬他那瘦巴巴的哥們，撇下你無所事事。

column1: (Besides, it looks like he's already found a date!)
column2: （況且看起來他已經找到約會對象了！）

column1: (You instantly take a liking to him.)
column2: （你立刻對他有好感。）

column1: Larry! Put that thing away! Lefty isn't interested in playing "match me for a drink" with you!
column2: 賴瑞！把那玩意收起來！李夫帝沒興趣跟你玩「賭酒」遊戲！

column1: Why not sit down at the bar first?
column2: 為啥不先在吧檯前坐下？

column1: You greet the bartender with a friendly "Hullo."
column2: 你用親切的「嗨」跟調酒師打招呼。

column1: I ain't sellin' no sissy drinks here, says Lefty. "Try again."
column2: 「我這兒不賣娘娘腔的飲料，」李夫帝說，「重來。」

column1: You mean to say you're playing "Leisure Suit Larry," and all you want is LIGHT beer??
column2: 你是說你在玩《閒散裝賴瑞》，卻只想喝「輕酒」？

column1: Are you sure? asks Lefty. "It'll cost ya $90.00!"
column2: 「你確定嗎？」李夫帝問，「要花你 90 塊！」

column1: I really want to, Mr. Lefty, you say, "but I just don't have 90 bucks!"
column2: 「我真的很想要啦，李夫帝先生，」你說，「但我根本沒有 90 塊！」

column1: Hey, everybody, cries Lefty, "Here comes a round from the last of the big time spenders!"
column2: 「嘿，各位，」李夫帝大喊，「一輪酒來啦，出自大豪客啦！」
